dataCards.title: 30-YR FIXED RATE
dataCards.insight: The 30-year fixed mortgage rate ticked up to 6.12%, reflecting a 5 basis point increase over the last week. Rates have climbed steadily since early May, driven by inflation concerns and Treasury yield fluctuations.
dataCards.takeaway: Lenders should prepare for rate-sensitive borrowers delaying decisions.
dataCards.context: Based on weekly Freddie Mac Primary Mortgage Market Survey data.

dataCards.title: 10-YR TREASURY YIELD
dataCards.insight: The 10-year Treasury yield stands at 4.05%, reflecting a 15 basis point drop from its peak earlier this week. Market volatility remains high as investors digest mixed inflation data and Fed guidance.
dataCards.takeaway: Monitor Treasury trends for near-term rate movement signals.
dataCards.context: Based on daily U.S. Treasury yield data.

dataCards.title: MBA APPLICATION INDEX
dataCards.insight: Refinance applications dropped 4% week-over-week, now comprising 38% of total activity. Purchase applications rose 2%, driven by strong demand in Sunbelt markets despite affordability challenges.
dataCards.takeaway: Focus on purchase lending opportunities in regional growth areas.
dataCards.context: Based on MBA's survey of mortgage bankers, brokers, and lenders.

dataCards.title: NAHB BUILDER CONFIDENCE
dataCards.insight: Builder confidence rose to an HMI of 55 in May, up from 52 in April. Builders report improving buyer traffic, though material costs and labor shortages remain headwinds.
dataCards.takeaway: Expect moderate increases in new housing starts this summer.
dataCards.context: Based on monthly NAHB/Wells Fargo Housing Market Index survey.

dataCards.title: RATE SNAPSHOT BY PRODUCT
dataCards.insight: 30-year fixed rates rose 4 bps to 6.12%, while 15-year fixed rates increased 2 bps to 5.45%. FHA and Jumbo products saw mixed movement, with FHA up 3 bps and Jumbo down 5 bps.
dataCards.takeaway: Tailor product offerings to align with rate-sensitive borrower needs.
dataCards.context: Based on Optimal Blue Mortgage Market Indices.

dataCards.title: EXISTING HOME SALES (SAAR)
dataCards.insight: Existing-home sales fell to a SAAR of 4.79M in April, down 8% year-over-year. Inventory remains tight at 2.7 months, while median prices rose 4.3% to $382,000.
dataCards.takeaway: Affordability pressures are limiting sales despite strong demand.
dataCards.context: Based on NAR's monthly report on existing-home sales.

headlines.title: CMLS Pushes DOJ/FTC for MLS Antitrust Guidance
headlines.fullContent: CMLS has urged regulators to recognize MLSs as pro-competitive entities, highlighting their role in providing data access to 1.7 million subscribers across 230 MLSs. The trade group argues that collaboration among MLSs benefits consumers and promotes market transparency.

headlines.title: New Home Sales Drop 6.2% in April
headlines.fullContent: New home sales fell to a SAAR of 622,000 in April, down 6.2% from March and 11.3% year-over-year. Median prices climbed to $422,500, while supply rose to 9.4 months, reflecting affordability challenges.

headlines.title: Mortgage Rates in the Sixes: A New Normal?
headlines.fullContent: Mortgage rates remain in the mid-6% range, reflecting ongoing volatility from inflation, geopolitical risks, and Fed policy uncertainty. Borrowers are adjusting expectations as rates stabilize at these elevated levels.

headlines.title: Fed Chair Faces Inflation Challenges Amid Rate Volatility
headlines.fullContent: New Fed Chair Kevin Warsh is navigating a complex economic landscape with inflation data complicating rate decisions. Analysts expect continued rate volatility as the Fed balances growth and price stability.
